questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: A short stem whose leaves are modified to perform the reproduction process in the plant.
questions.a_txt: Flower

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: A leaf which the vegetative bud emerges from its axil, and develops into a flower.
questions.a_txt: Bract

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: A cluster of flowers carried by the peduncle.
questions.a_txt: Inflorescence

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: The part of the stem that carries a cluster of flowers.
questions.a_txt: Peduncle

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: An inflorescence that resembles a cluster of grapes, in which the flowers bloom from bottom to top.
questions.a_txt: Simple raceme inflorescence

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: An inflorescence in which all the pedicels emerge from one point at the end of the peduncle.
questions.a_txt: Simple umbel inflorescence

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: An inflorescence that resembles a comb, where the flowers emerge on both sides of the peduncle.
questions.a_txt: Simple corymb inflorescence

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: A swollen part at the end of the flower pedicel that carries the floral whorls.
questions.a_txt: Receptacle

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: The floral whorl whose function is to protect the inner parts of the flower, especially before blooming.
questions.a_txt: Calyx

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: The floral whorl whose function is attracting insects by its colour and fragrant.
questions.a_txt: Corolla

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: A flower in which sepals and petals are absent.
questions.a_txt: Naked flowers

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: The floral whorl that consists of a group of carpels.
questions.a_txt: Pistil

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: An inflammation caused by an immune response to harmless substances such as pollen grains.
questions.a_txt: Hay fever

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: The flower whose floral leaves are arranged in four floral whorls.
questions.a_txt: Typical flower

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: The flower that contains both male and female organs together.
questions.a_txt: Bisexual flower

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements:
questions.q_txt: The flower that has either male organs only or female organs only.
questions.a_txt: Unisexual flower

questions.ex_title: Identify the odd item in each list, then mention the connection between the others:
questions.q_txt: Calyx / Corolla / Stem / Stamen / Pistil.
questions.a_txt: Odd item: Stem. Connection: The floral whorls of the typical flower.

questions.ex_title: Identify the odd item in each list, then mention the connection between the others:
questions.q_txt: Sepals / Petals / Tubers / Carpels.
questions.a_txt: Odd item: Tubers. Connection: The floral whorl leaves of the female flower.

questions.ex_title: Identify the odd item in each list, then mention the connection between the others:
questions.q_txt: Stigma / Stamen / Style / Ovary.
questions.a_txt: Odd item: Stamen. Connection: Components of the carpel.

questions.q_txt: Palm / Maize (corn) / Tomato / Pumpkin.
questions.a_txt: Odd item: Tomato. Connection: Unisexual flowers.

questions.ex_title: What is meant by each of the following:
questions.q_txt: The flower.
questions.a_txt: A short stem whose leaves are modified to perform the reproductive function.

questions.ex_title: What is meant by each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Bract.
questions.a_txt: A leaf where the vegetative bud emerges from its axil and develops into a flower.

questions.ex_title: What is meant by each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Inflorescence.
questions.a_txt: A cluster of flowers carried by the peduncle.

questions.ex_title: What is meant by each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Peduncle.
questions.a_txt: The part of the stem that carries a cluster of flowers.

questions.ex_title: What is meant by each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Receptacle.
questions.a_txt: A swollen part at the end of the flower pedicel that carries the floral leaves (whorls).

questions.ex_title: What is meant by each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Hay fever.
questions.a_txt: It is an inflammation caused by an immune response to harmless substances such as pollen grains.

questions.ex_title: What is meant by each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Typical flower.
questions.a_txt: The flower whose floral leaves are arranged in four floral whorls.

questions.ex_title: What is meant by each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Bisexual flower.
questions.a_txt: The flower that contains both male and female organs.

questions.ex_title: What is meant by each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Unisexual flower.
questions.a_txt: The flower that has either male organs only or female organs only.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance of each of the following:
questions.q_txt: The flower.
questions.a_txt: The sexual reproductive organ in flowering plants.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance of each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Receptacle.
questions.a_txt: Carrying the floral (whorl) leaves.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance of each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Calyx.
questions.a_txt: Protection of the inner parts of the flower, especially before blooming.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance of each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Corolla.
questions.a_txt: Protection of the reproductive organs of the flower. Attracting insects that play an important role in completing the process of floral reproduction.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance of each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Stamens.
questions.a_txt: Production of male gametes (pollen grains).

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance of each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Pistil.
questions.a_txt: Production of female gametes (ovules).

questions.ex_title: Give reasons for the following:
questions.q_txt: Naming the simple raceme inflorescence as such.
questions.a_txt: Because the flowers emerge from all directions and each has a small stalk, (pedicel) giving it a shape similar to a cluster of grapes.

questions.ex_title: Give reasons for the following:
questions.q_txt: The inflorescences of the iberis plant are called simple corymb inflorescences.
questions.a_txt: Because the flowers emerge on both sides of the peduncle and all are almost at the same level, giving it a shape similar to a comb.

questions.ex_title: Give reasons for the following:
questions.q_txt: The inflorescences of the onion plant are simple umbel inflorescences.
questions.a_txt: Because the flowers emerge from one point at the end of the peduncle and their pedicels are of equal length, giving it a shape similar to a tent or an umbrella.

questions.ex_title: Give reasons for the following:
questions.q_txt: The importance of sepals for the flower especially before blooming.
questions.a_txt: Because they protect the inner parts of the flower.

questions.ex_title: Give reasons for the following:
questions.q_txt: The calyx and corolla whorls in the petunia plant are not called the perianth.
questions.a_txt: Because in Petunia flowers, it is easy to distinguish the colour of the sepals from the petals.

questions.ex_title: Give reasons for the following:
questions.q_txt: The stamen is the male organ in the flower.
questions.a_txt: Because it produces male gametes (pollen grains).

questions.ex_title: Give reasons for the following:
questions.q_txt: The pistil is the female organ in the flower.
questions.a_txt: Because it produces female gametes (ovules).

questions.ex_title: Give reasons for the following:
questions.q_txt: The petals of rose plant have an economic importance for human.
questions.a_txt: Because it is used in the production of rose water and rose oil.

questions.ex_title: Give reasons for the following:
questions.q_txt: It is preferable to avoid the areas where the concentrations of pollen grains are high.
questions.a_txt: For the prevention of hay fever, which causes frequent sneezing and watery eyes.

questions.ex_title: Give reasons for the following:
questions.q_txt: The tomato flower is a typical bisexual flower.
questions.a_txt: Because it contains the four floral whorls and contains both the male organ (stamens) and the female organ (pistil).

questions.ex_title: Mention one difference between each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Stock flower and iberis plant.
questions.a_txt: Stock flower: Has simple raceme inflorescences. Iberis plant: Has simple corymb inflorescences.

questions.ex_title: Mention one difference between each of the following:
questions.q_txt: Daffodil flower and willow flower.
questions.a_txt: Daffodil flower: It is difficult to distinguish the colour of the sepals from the colour of the petals in it. Willow flower: Lacks both sepals and petals.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer the following:
questions.q_txt: From the opposite figures (1, 2, 3): (1) What do the figures (1) to (3) represent? (2) Mention two examples for each of them.
questions.a_txt: (1) Figure (1): Simple raceme inflorescence. Figure (2): Simple corymb inflorescence. Figure (3): Simple umbel inflorescence. (2) Figure (1): Snapdragon plant / Stock plant. Figure (2): Cherry plant / Iberis plant. Figure (3): Garlic plant / Onion plant.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer the following:
questions.q_txt: The two opposite figures are two different flowers: (1) What are the similarities and differences between the flower (1) and the flower (2)? (2) What is the economic importance of the flower (1)?
questions.a_txt: (1) Similarity: Both are flowering plants. Difference: Figure (1): The sepals are separated and the petals are separated, arranged in several whorls. Figure (2): The sepals are fused and the petals are fused, arranged in one whorl. (2) The petals of Egyptian rose are used in the production of rose water and rose oil.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer the following:
questions.q_txt: From the opposite diagram: (1) Write the labels indicated by the numbers. (2) What is the name of the floral whorl in which each of the following parts participate in its formation: 1- Part (3). 2- Part (4). (3) What is the name of the organ that consists of: 1- The two parts (5), (6). 2- The parts (7), (8), (9). (4) Which numbers represent the parts forming the floral whorl that is not present in the flower with the symbol ♂?
questions.a_txt: (1) (1): Pedicel, (2): Receptacle, (3): Sepal, (4): Petal, (5): Filament, (6): Anther, (7): Stigma, (8): Style, (9): Ovary, (10): Ovules. (2) 1- Calyx, 2- Corolla. (3) 1- Stamen, 2- Carpel. (4) (7), (8), (9), (10).

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer the following:
questions.q_txt: These figures represent four different flowers: (1) What is the similarity between each of the following: 1- The flower (1) and the flower (2). 2- The flower (2) and the flower (4). (2) What is the difference between each of the following: 1- The flower (1) and the flower (2). 2- The flower (3) and the flower (4).
questions.a_txt: (1) 1- Both carry the female organ (pistil). 2- Both carry the male organ (stamens). (2) 1- The flower (1) is unisexual (female), while the flower (2) is bisexual. 2- The flower (3) is female, while the flower (4) is male.

questions.ex_title: Variant questions:
questions.q_txt: Arrange the floral whorls in the typical flower from outside to inside.
questions.a_txt: First: Calyx, Second: Corolla, Third: Stamens, Fourth: Pistil.

questions.ex_title: Variant questions:
questions.q_txt: The medical benefits of flowers for human are numerous ... Mention some examples to explain this statement.
questions.a_txt: The flowers of chamomile are used to calm the nervous system, while the flowers of hibiscus are used to lower high blood pressure.
